recipes.title: Maqluba
recipes.description: Upside-down rice cake of layered lamb, fried aubergine, cauliflower and potatoes, flipped onto a platter at the table.
steps: Brown the lamb with onion, cardamom and salt then simmer for 60 minutes; reserve the broth.
Slice aubergine, cauliflower and potatoes; salt the aubergine and pat dry.
Deep-fry each vegetable in turn until golden, draining on paper.
In a heavy pot layer tomatoes, then lamb, then fried vegetables in colorful tiers.
Top with rinsed rice, allspice and turmeric and pour in 1 liter of hot broth.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes, rest 15 minutes, invert onto a platter and scatter with toasted pine nuts.

recipes.title: Musakhan
recipes.description: Sumac-roasted chicken on taboon bread soaked with caramelized onions and pine nuts, the national dish of Palestine.
steps: Joint the chicken into 8 pieces and rub with allspice, cinnamon, lemon juice and salt.
Slice the onions thinly and sauté in olive oil for 25 minutes until very soft and just golden.
Stir in 4 tablespoons of sumac and cook another minute, then remove from heat.
Roast the chicken on a tray at 200°C for 35 minutes until cooked and crisp-skinned.
Lay the taboon bread on a platter, cover with the sumac onions and chicken pieces.
Toast pine nuts in a little olive oil, scatter on top and dust with the remaining sumac.

recipes.title: Maftoul
recipes.description: Hand-rolled Palestinian couscous tossed with tender chicken and chickpeas in a warmly spiced cinnamon broth.
steps: Brown the chicken pieces in olive oil with sliced onion, then add water and cinnamon stick.
Simmer for 50 minutes until tender, then strain and reserve 800 ml of broth.
Steam the maftoul over the gently simmering broth for 25 minutes until plump.
Toss the steamed maftoul with olive oil, allspice and salt.
Warm the chickpeas in some broth with cumin and turmeric.
Pile the maftoul on a platter, arrange the chicken and chickpeas on top and ladle hot broth around.

recipes.title: Mansaf
recipes.description: Slow-cooked lamb in a tangy fermented yogurt sauce, served on a bed of rice and shrak bread, eaten communally.
steps: Soak the jameed overnight in water, then blend smooth and reserve.
Brown the lamb pieces in ghee with onion and cardamom, then cover with water and simmer 1.5 hours.
Whisk the jameed slurry into the simmering broth, stirring constantly to prevent splitting.
Cook rinsed rice in some of the strained broth with allspice and turmeric until fluffy.
Lay the shrak bread on a wide platter, spoon over rice, then arrange lamb pieces and ladle yogurt sauce.
Toast pine nuts and almonds in ghee and scatter on top before serving with extra sauce on the side.

recipes.title: Qidra
recipes.description: Hebron's celebrated rice and lamb dish, slow-cooked in a clay pot with chickpeas, garlic and warm spices.
steps: Layer the lamb at the bottom of a heavy clay or metal pot.
Scatter chickpeas and whole peeled garlic cloves around the lamb.
Add rinsed rice, spices, salt and ghee and pour over hot water.
Seal the pot tightly with foil and a lid and bake at 160°C for 2.5 hours.
Open the lid carefully and check the rice is fluffy and the lamb falling apart.
Tip onto a platter and serve with plain yogurt.

recipes.title: Knafeh Nabulsieh
recipes.description: Nablus's signature golden cheese pastry, with a layer of stretchy white cheese under a crisp orange semolina crust.
steps: Soak the akkawi cheese in cold water for 6 hours, changing it every 2 hours.
Boil sugar, water and lemon juice for 8 minutes, stir in orange blossom water and chill.
Pulse the kataifi with semolina until fine, then mix with melted ghee and orange coloring.
Press the buttered pastry firmly into a 30 cm tray as a tight even base.
Cover with sliced cheese and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until the bottom is deep gold.
Invert immediately onto a tray, douse with cold syrup and garnish with pistachios.

recipes.title: Mloukhieh
recipes.description: Soupy stew of finely chopped jute mallow leaves with chicken and garlic, served with rice and toasted bread.
steps: Simmer the chicken with salt and a peeled garlic clove for 60 minutes; strain the broth.
Chop the mloukhieh leaves very finely with a sharp knife.
Bring 1 liter of strained broth back to a simmer and add the leaves.
Mince the remaining garlic with the fresh coriander and fry in olive oil until fragrant.
Stir the garlic-coriander mixture (taqliya) into the simmering soup with cumin and lemon juice.
Cook gently for 15 minutes and serve with shredded chicken, rice and toasted pita.

recipes.title: Rummaniyya
recipes.description: Gazan lentil and aubergine stew brightened with sour pomegranate juice, tahini and a final tarka of garlic and chili.
steps: Simmer the rinsed lentils in water with salt for 25 minutes until tender.
Cube the aubergines and fry in olive oil until deep gold; drain on paper.
Stir aubergines and pomegranate juice into the lentils and cook 15 minutes.
Whisk in the tahini and a little water to loosen if needed.
Crush the garlic, fry in olive oil with cumin and Aleppo pepper for 1 minute, then stir into the stew.
Finish with chopped dill and serve warm with bread.

recipes.title: Zibdiyit Gambari
recipes.description: Gazan clay-pot prawn stew of tomato, dill, chili and garlic, baked until bubbling and served straight from the dish.
steps: Pound the garlic and chili with salt to a rough paste in a mortar.
Grate the tomatoes into a bowl, discarding the skins.
Heat olive oil in a clay pot, fry the garlic-chili paste for 1 minute.
Add tomato pulp, cumin and salt and simmer 10 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the prawns and cook 4-5 minutes until just pink.
Top with chopped dill and lemon juice and serve straight from the pot with bread.

recipes.title: Sumagiyya
recipes.description: Hearty Gazan stew of lamb, chard and chickpeas thickened with sumac juice, tahini and dill — eaten on holidays.
steps: Soak the sumac in 500 ml hot water for 1 hour, then strain to extract the tart red juice.
Brown the lamb in olive oil and simmer with water and salt for 75 minutes.
Add chickpeas and chopped chard stems and simmer 10 minutes more.
Stir in chard leaves, the sumac juice, tahini and Aleppo pepper.
Crush garlic with cumin, fry in olive oil for 1 minute and tip into the stew.
Finish with chopped dill and rest 10 minutes before serving with bread.

recipes.title: Shorabat Adas
recipes.description: Velvety yellow lentil soup brightened with cumin and lemon, a Levantine staple at iftar tables.
steps: Sauté chopped onion, carrot and garlic in olive oil for 5 minutes.
Stir in the rinsed lentils, cumin and turmeric and toast for 1 minute.
Pour in the water and salt and bring to a steady simmer.
Cook for 25 minutes until the lentils have completely broken down.
Blend the soup with an immersion blender until silky.
Stir in lemon juice and ladle into bowls with extra cumin and toasted bread.

recipes.title: Mahashi Waraq Dawali
recipes.description: Palestinian-style stuffed grape leaves, rolled tightly with rice, lamb and tomato and simmered in lemon broth.
steps: Blanch vine leaves briefly to soften, then drain and trim stems.
Mix rinsed rice with lamb, diced tomato, tomato paste, allspice, cinnamon and salt.
Place a teaspoon of filling on each leaf and roll tightly into thin cigars.
Layer rolls in a heavy pot lined with extra leaves; weight with a heatproof plate.
Pour over olive oil, lemon juice and water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook for 50 minutes until the rice is tender and the broth is glossy.
